titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 731

p.seg: To all Christ's faithful to whom the present writing shall have come, Clarebald of Ashby sends greeting.

Know you all that I have let at perpetual ferme, to God, blessed Cuthbert, Saint Ebbe, and the monks of Durham in perpetuity serving God at Coldingham, two fisheries on the Tweed, that is, one below the garden at Fishwick and the other at Shipswell, which my lord Walter Oliphant gave to me and my heirs in return for my homage and service; I have also granted to the said monks the said fisheries to be held of me and my heirs in perpetuity, free and quit from every custom, exaction, and demand, except for eight shillings which the said monks shall pay me and my heirs at two terms each year in return for everything, that is, two shillings at the feast of Saint John the Baptist and four shillings at the feast of Saint Martin in the winter. I and my heirs shall warrant the said fisheries to the said monks against all men and make the said monks quit of every forinsec service both towards the lord king and towards my lord and his heirs.
In testimonty of this matter I have applied my seal to this writing.
